data.Name: Void
data.Geometry_Description: Void Characters are a composite of all other numbers, and a reflection of nothingness as well. They have infinite potential, at least unless some other force acts upon them.
data.Chi: Gain Chi when you encourage others to join you, speak, or are inclusive.
data.Yang: Gain Yang by acting knowlegeable, even if they know nothing.
data.Yin: Gain Yin when they act unambitious, uninterested or bored.
data.Stress: Gain Stress when you are tested, examined, studied, measured or compete against others (allowing ranking to take place).
data.Goal_Description: They define their goals only to ignore them, they may work towards some goal, only to abandon it later.
data.Gift_Description: They are powerful, full of limitless potential, and seem indefatigable. They add +1 Success Level to any action that requires energy, activity or motion.

Proficiencies.Name: Void
Proficiencies.Description: An empty space, often left after something has fallen away, a complete lack of anything, something ineffective, unenforceable or not binding.

Proficiencies.Name: Potential
Proficiencies.Description: A measure of possibility, or something that isn't true, but has a possibility of becoming true. Often used to describe a latent skill or ability that may be developed.

data.Name: Circle
data.Geometry_Description: Circle Characters are determined innovators, creative-thinkers and natural loners and are often perceived as leaders.
data.Chi: Gain Chi by acting confidently, boldly, or alone.
data.Yang: Gain Yang by acting self-important, domineering or courageous.
data.Yin: Gain Yin by acting self-critical, stubborn, or innovative.
data.Stress: Gain Stress when you follow orders or do what you are told.
data.Goal_Description: They define their goals from their Personality (or Personalities), usually this is based on their Motivations and is usually expressed as a Quest of the same Facet. An Idol character, for example, motivated by a desire to be adored, may make it their personal quest to brave some famous peril, or charm the most people they can (by becoming famous).
data.Gift_Description: They are determined people. They add +1 Success Level on any Action where determination, resolve, self-sacrifice, fixation or stubbornness may help.

data.Name: Half-Moon
data.Geometry_Description: Half-Moon Characters are co-operative, harmony seeking diplomats, but they can become manipulative and petty.
data.Chi: Gain Chi by co-operating with others (including following orders), acting diplomatic, or patient.
data.Yang: Gain Yang by acting sensitive, petty, or detail-orientated.
data.Yin: Gain Yin by acting intuitive, childish, or supportive.
data.Goal_Description: They are dedicated to the goals of a group or pact. Sometimes they will even dedicate themselves to helping another Character achieve their personal Goals. Their goals will chance and shift as they join and leave groups, and make or lose allies.
data.Gift_Description: The Half-Moon Geometry are sensitive, diplomatic people. They add +1 Success Level on any Action where they are working with others.

data.Name: Triangle
data.Geometry_Description: Triangle Characters are intelligent, creative, expressive, often artistic types. They love to talk, being entertaining and all ways have a story to tell, but it may turn gossipy and superficial.
data.Chi: Gain Chi when you communicate, express yourself, or tell a story.
data.Yang: Gain Yang by acting optimistic, creatively, intelligently, or by exaggerating.
data.Yin: Gain Yin by acting emotionally volatile, superficial, insincere, or by sharing gossip.
data.Stress: Gain Stress when someone will not listen to you.
data.Goal_Description: Their goals are based upon their Core(s), usually defined as a Quest that opposes their Core Facet's Quest. For example, a Wielder Core (Burden) does not themselves have an Obtain Quest, their Goal instead is to protect or guard the Descendant they wield and keep some enemy or rival from Obtaining it.
data.Gift_Description: The Triangle Geometry makes people who are witty, quicker thinkers. They add +1 Success Level to any Action where speed, wit, agility, swiftness or rapid thinking may be a helpful factor.

data.Name: Square
data.Geometry_Description: Square Characters are dependable, trustworthy and hard-working, but can be rigid and narrow-minded.
data.Chi: Gain Chi when you are productive, helpful, or conservative.
data.Yang: Gain Yang by building, acting rigid, or bossy.
data.Yin: Gain Yin by complaining, thinking literally or working hard.
data.Stress: Gain Stress when you take a Failure Level from any source.
data.Goal_Description: They prefer social goals, like making many friends, being a good member of society, helping those in need, supporting the local community. Such goals can often easily accommodate group goals and the goals of friends.
data.Gift_Description: The Square Geometry makes people more stable. They add +1 Success Level to any Action where stability, strength, security, constancy, steadiness, or maturity may be a factor.

data.Name: Pentagon
data.Geometry_Description: Pentagon Characters are the freedom-loving risk-takers, they may be fearless, or foolish.
data.Chi: Gain Chi by acting brave, reckless, or by having fun.
data.Yang: Gain Yang by acting rebellious, trying to effect change, or emotional.
data.Yin: Gain Yin by acting resilient, flexible or foolish.
data.Stress: Gain Stress when they make any commitment.
data.Goal_Description: These Characters are only interesting in having a great story to tell at the end. They leap at any chance to test, prove, or improve themselves, and this will often land them neck-deep in a narrative that will sweep them along.
data.Gift_Description: The Pentagon Geometry are more adventurous than others. They add +1 Success Level on any Action that is risk-taking, rash, reckless, potentially hazardous, brave or foolhardy.

data.Name: Hexagon
data.Geometry_Description: The Hexagon are as hard working and responsible as the Square, but with more wit and creativity. They tend to think on the big picture, rather than the details.
data.Chi: Gain Chi by nurturing others (which may be seen as meddling and include giving them Chi, or making food), or being supportive, or by dealing with the big-picture.
data.Yang: Gain Yang by acting sensible, responsible, or self-important.
data.Yin: Gain Yin by acting romantic, sensual, or self-indulgent.
data.Stress: Gain Stress if you do not do something to help when you can.
data.Goal_Description: They are likely to set themselves the most ambitious, grand and lofty goals. These are the sort to declare they will be Emperor of Known Space, or change the world somehow. Sometimes they even manage it.
data.Gift_Description: The Hexagon Character is often a visionary, this might mean they see actual visions, or that they have a very developed imagination. They add +1 Success Level to any action to do with perception, prophecy or visual arts.

data.Name: Heptagon
data.Geometry_Description: The Heptagon (sometimes Septagon) Character is the intellectual, but intuitive, deep — to the point of over — thinker. They can be wise, but may become aloof.
data.Chi: Gain Chi by making guesses (positing theories) or plans, asking questions, and analysing things.
data.Yang: Gain Yang by changing your mind, being proved right, or being pessimistic.
data.Yin: Gain Yin by being intuitive, being proved wrong, or acting aloof.
data.Goal_Description: They set Deep goals for themselves, these may be grave, serious, sincere, intense, heartfelt, abstruse, artful, obscure or profound goals that often give deeper meaning to the Character's life, and mainly express as a broadly educational, spiritual, magical, technological, charitable, scientific or artistic pursuit.
data.Gift_Description: The Heptagon Geometry makes people wise, they are deep thinkers, philosophical, knowledgeable, but intuitive and spiritual as well. They add +1 Success Level to any Action where wisdom, knowledge, logic, calculation, or raw intellect may be a factor.

data.Name: Octagon
data.Geometry_Description: Octagon Characters are powerful, ambitious, but ultimately materialistic individuals.
data.Chi: Gain Chi by acting disciplined, materialistic, or ambitious.
data.Yang: Gain Yang by acting rebellious, bossy, or generous.
data.Yin: Gain Yin by acting opinionated, relaxed, or persevering.
data.Stress: Gain Stress when you take a Failure Level from any source.
data.Goal_Description: These characters are invested in preserving the status quo, they dislike change, and like things they way they are. Their goals are usually purely material and are always a reflection of the status quo, personal goals include riches, luxuriessafety, security and purely physiological needs, such as food, clothing or shelter.
data.Gift_Description: The Octagon Geometry makes people better at physically manifesting their dreams, this can make them into powerful politicians, business people, cut-throats and thieves or even feared magicians. They add +1 Success Level to any Action to do with the physical, material manifestation of their dreams, schemes, and desires.

data.Name: Nonagon
data.Geometry_Description: Nonagon Characters are deeply spiritual people, intuitive, compassionate and caring, they are natural humanitarians, and passionate fighters against injustice, especially in the status quo.
data.Chi: Gain Chi by acting generous, compassionate, or idealistic.
data.Yang: Gain Yang by arguing, acting passionate or knowledgeable.
data.Yin: Gain Yin by agreeing with another or acting Big-headed/arrogant or submissive.
data.Stress: Gain Stress when you percieve another being harmed.
data.Goal_Description: They are driven to save human lives, alleviate pain and suffering, and generally help people. They may change the world, reform society, and will succeed, die or become rich and famous trying.
data.Gift_Description: The Nonagon Geometry makes Characters more spiritual, more intuitive and compassionate. They feel deep, strong connections with others (and even things and places), and this can cause them to rise to protect, help, aid and defend others from injustice and harm. They add +1 Success Level to any Action where their Spirit or Passion may help, especially aiding, helping, protecting or defending others or fighting injustice.

data.Name: Decagon
data.Geometry_Description: Decagon Characters are like Circle Characters turned up to, well 10. They are more independent than Circles, but also work better with others.
data.Chi: Gain Chi when you act alone, or for the good of others.
data.Yang: Gain Yang by acting responsible, sincere or dogmatic.
data.Yin: Gain Yin by acting loyal, determined, or intolerant.
data.Stress: Gain Stress when affected by any emotion.
data.Goal_Description: These Characters set impossible, absurd, impractical goals that they can fail at over and over again, each time achieving something more. These Goals are often set by the Character's Personality, but are amplified in scale, scope and difficulty to be practically unachievable. For example, an Idol could choose to brave a truly terrible threat, or to charm everyone, the whole world.
data.Gift_Description: The Decagon Geometry makes people determined to the point of working themselves to the bone to achieve their goals. They add +2 Success Levels to any Action but take a Flesh Wound for that resolve.

data.Name: Undecagon
data.Geometry_Description: Undecagon Characters are mystically amplified Half-Moon Characters (they are a 2 turned up to 11!). Undecagon Characters can be so intuitive as to even believe themselves Psychic, and even prove it to some.
data.Chi: Gain Chi by co-operating with others or being patient, knowledgeable, or vague.
data.Yang: Gain Yang by acting sensitive, disciplined, or intelligent.
data.Yin: Gain Yin by acting psychic, immature or altruistic.
data.Stress: Gain Stress when you are specific or proved wrong.
data.Goal_Description: The Character has secrets, and one of the things they keep secret is what their own goals are. Often their secret goals may seem to ally with another's, but often this alliance will be a one way street, with the Secret Goal, or just the secrecy itself, eventually undermining the pact. Sometimes a character’s secret goal may simply be to keep a secret, if this is so, it is rarely their own secret they are keeping, but someone else’s important secret.
data.Gift_Description: The Undecagon Geometry is so about intuition, and so in tune with the world around them, that it makes people seem psychic. They add +1 Success Level on any Action where they might be able to claim Psychic assistance, resonance or similar.

data.Name: Dodecagon
data.Geometry_Description: Dodecagon Characters are something of an intersection of circles, half-moons and triangles, they are gifted communicators and creative types, but with strong social ties that off-set rugged independence.
data.Chi: Gain Chi when you cooperate with others, or act creatively.
data.Yang: Gain Yang by arguing, fighting with or insulting others.
data.Yin: Gain Yin by agreeing with others, asking their opinon, or following them.
data.Stress: Gain Stress when you are unable to make your point.
data.Goal_Description: The Character has Melded goals, that incorporate their Personality, Group and Core goals, and except when they are diametrically opposed, they incorporate the goals of their companions into their own goals. Sometimes when opposed by someone in the group they will simply pause that goal while the opposing person is around.
data.Gift_Description: The Dodecagon Geometry makes characters extremely communicative. In fact, they may add +1 Success Levels to any attempt to communicate with another character, this weirdly rises to + 2 Success Levels if the Dodecagon and the target do not share a language.

data.Name: Triskaidecagon
data.Geometry_Description: Triskaidecagon Characters are a sort of dark reflection of the stability and social nature of the Square.
data.Chi: Gain Chi when you are focused, pragmatic, or evasive.
data.Yang: Gain Yang by acting independent, assured, or determined.
data.Yin: Gain Yin by acting diligent, pedantic or by complaining.
data.Stress: Gain Stress whenever outcomes are uncertain, or unobvious.
data.Goal_Description: They set themselves long and complex goals that have many steps and many contingencies. Their plans always assume luck will be factor (usually working against them) and they break huge goals down into smaller manageable tasks. Because of this, they are often focused on achieving the next step in their complex plan. They can appear capricious or mercurial as their goals seem to shift and change as the steps unfold, but know that there is always a much greater plan in motion in their head.
data.Gift_Description:
The Triskaidecagon Geometry makes people have extreme luck. Sadly you can never tell if the luck will be good or bad. On any Action they may roll a 1d6 Luck dice.
RollResult
1+2 Failure Levels
2-3+1 Failure Level
4-5+1 Success Level
6+2 Success Levels
